title: AT Field questions
wikitext:
[]: AT Field questions             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:46 pm When the Evas and Angels unfold their At fields, the visual is a wall of hexagons (octogons, whatever. not-important). Is the At field unidirectional when fully unfolded, or not? Sachiel was able to block most of the JSSDF's missiles when the field wasn't properly unfolded, and yet projected it in one direction when Eva-01 went apeshit. Shamshel, Ramiel, Gaghiel, Israfel (iirc) and Zeruel all pulled something similar . Thoughts?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 29, 2009 12:58 pm The AT field generally only becomes visible when stressed. However I think it's generally implied that the field is omni-directional. It's kind of vague though. They never took enough time to explain the mechanics of it.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 29, 2009 1:35 pm If you have ever played Halo 3 and you know of the "Bubble Shield" well try to think of it as that...Kinda.

[]:             Quote Post by VoidEater » Wed Jul 29, 2009 1:36 pm 1) ATF can be whatever the plot requires (someone's gonna say it...) 2) The ATF field holds LCL in a particular shape, and holds others away from the heart. So, we know it can be omnidirectional. It can also be specifically projected for effects like shields, weapons, etc. Rather versatile. We might posit that the mechanism that holds or directs or moves a deployed ATF is also a part of the ATF.

[]: Re: AT Field questions             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 29, 2009 3:17 pm CyberXIII wrote: When the Evas and Angels unfold their At fields, the visual is a wall of hexagons (octogons, whatever. not-important).

[]: Re: AT Field questions             Quote Post by Allemann » Wed Jul 29, 2009 3:38 pm Reichu wrote: The A.T. Field (in the super-humans, anyway) basically amounts to telekinetic voodoo.

[]: Re: AT Field questions             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 29, 2009 4:30 pm Reichu wrote: Wonder why none of the Angels have tried a 3D version of the ATF barrier yet...

[]: Re: AT Field questions             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Wed Jul 29, 2009 4:58 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Reichu wrote: Wonder why none of the Angels have tried a 3D version of the ATF barrier yet...

[]: Re: AT Field questions             Quote Post by schismatics » Wed Jul 29, 2009 5:40 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Reichu wrote: Wonder why none of the Angels have tried a 3D version of the ATF barrier yet...

[]:             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Thu Jul 30, 2009 7:24 pm I thought I'd say some words about the AT Field that no one else has mentioned. Kaoru: "The AT Field is a sacred barrier of the soul, in which no one may intrude." (Episode 24). In other words, it separates peoples' minds from each other. So when giant Rei spread her AT Field around the entire world, it destroyed everyone Else's, so that everyone turned into LCL, and fused together.

[]:             Quote Post by TheLobe » Fri Jul 31, 2009 8:46 am I always thought that the field is only shown that way because that's where it hit. but looking back as they have to expand it I'd assume that's how they make it.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Jul 31, 2009 10:57 am Second question. When the Angels attack, do they have to lower their ATF's in order to do so? I ask, because I can't help but notice that Sachiel never attacked while simultaneously protecting itself with his ATF. Zeruel likewise only skullfucked Unit-00 after Rei had torn open his ATF.

[]:             Quote Post by Rabid-Sachiel » Fri Jul 31, 2009 12:20 pm No, I don't think they do. Since it's their AT Field, I doubt they'd have to temporarily stop using their AT Field when they attack. If it is theirs, then it probably wouldn't give them such a disadvantage. Also, remember, the AT Field can usually only be seen when it's stressed/being attacked.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Fri Jul 31, 2009 8:58 pm Yes, but I couldn't help but notice that Sachiel and Zeruel only attacked when the ATF wasn't visible/ unfolded, or torn open. Oh, and when the ATF is torn open/ neutralized by another force, is it possible to close it back up? Zeruel seemed unable to do so when Unit 01 sliced his in half, Sachiel was likewise unable to close his when Eva-01 tore it open.

[]:             Quote Post by TheWaffleCult » Fri Jul 31, 2009 11:12 pm I was always under the impression that the AT field usually took the form of its user normally - i.e. fitting to our shape. That's what I always gathered from the Gendo/Rei interaction in End of Evangelion. Manipulating the shape of the AT Field made sense as something only Angels and highly-trained pilots could do.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Aug 01, 2009 8:46 am Rabid-Sachiel wrote: So when giant Rei spread her AT Field around the entire world,

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Sun Aug 16, 2009 6:18 pm When an Angel Attacks, does it have to drop the AT Field when it does so? I suppose this applies to the Evas too...

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 16, 2009 6:55 pm CyberXIII wrote: When an Angel Attacks, does it have to drop the AT Field when it does so? I suppose this applies to the Evas too...

[]:             Quote Post by Legendary » Sun Aug 16, 2009 8:06 pm For more conventional attacks, Ramiel's stays up even when it does fire its lasers.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Sun Aug 16, 2009 8:19 pm Well...I seem to recall at least one instance of Shogoki deploying, expanding her AT Field (shimmer), then immediantly opeing fire...if I recall correctly, this seems to indicate it imposes to resistance to its owner...or something...
